description: Core migration: create online_meetings table (PK (tenant, meeting_id), all columns per PRD), distributed on tenant with transaction:false Citus helper pattern.
online_meetings status CHECK includes scheduled, ended, recording_pending, recording_ready, no_recording, cancelled, failed.
online_meetings unique (tenant, provider, provider_meeting_id) and indexes on (tenant, interaction_id), (tenant, appointment_request_id), (tenant, status, end_time).

Core migration: create online_meeting_artifacts child table (PK (tenant, artifact_id), artifact_type, provider_artifact_id, content_url, document_id, file_id, created_date_time), colocated with online_meetings.
online_meeting_artifacts unique (tenant, meeting_id, artifact_type, provider_artifact_id) for idempotent upserts; index (tenant, meeting_id).
Both new tables apply and roll back cleanly on plain Postgres (no Citus) and on Citus.
Core migration: insert 'Online Meeting' system interaction type with icon 'video' (INSERT-only, follows add_general_interaction_type pattern).
Types: add IOnlineMeeting and IOnlineMeetingArtifact (with artifacts[] on IOnlineMeeting), exported from @alga-psa/types barrel.
Types: add optional read-only online_meeting?: IOnlineMeeting | null to IInteraction.
OnlineMeetingModel (session-agnostic, createTenantKnex-based): create, getById (with artifacts), getByProviderMeetingId, getByInteractionId, getByAppointmentRequestId, update, listPendingRecordings.
OnlineMeetingModel artifact helpers: upsertArtifact (idempotent on unique key), listArtifacts(meetingId).
InteractionModel.addInteraction/getById accept an optional trx (default createTenantKnex), fixing the existing gap where the model opened its own connection outside the action transaction.

Shared create-interaction helper replicates the action's status defaulting, contact->client resolution, INTERACTION_LOGGED + INTERACTION_CREATED publishing, and revalidatePath; both addInteraction action and meeting paths call it.
onlineMeetingActions: getOnlineMeetingForInteraction (withAuth).
onlineMeetingActions: refreshMeetingRecordings(meetingId) (withAuth) invoking the shared fetch handler.
EE: change createTeamsMeeting to create a calendar-backed event (POST /users/{organizerUpn}/events, isOnlineMeeting:true, onlineMeetingProvider:teamsForBusiness); capture event id.
EE: resolve the onlineMeeting id from the event joinUrl (filter on JoinWebUrl, URL-encoded) for later artifact calls.
EE: appointment-approval meetings created with NO external attendees; MSP-initiated meetings MAY include attendees.
EE: updateTeamsMeeting and deleteTeamsMeeting move to the events endpoints (PATCH/DELETE /users/{organizerUpn}/events/{eventId}).
Facade CreateTeamsMeetingResult (and EE return) extended with organizerUpn, organizerUserId (AAD object id), and eventId; persisted on online_meetings.

EE fetchMeetingArtifacts: GET .../users/{organizerUserId}/onlineMeetings/{id}/recordings and /transcripts (collections) + transcript content (Accept: text/vtt); URL-encoded path segments; returns content URLs not clickable links.
Add fetchMeetingArtifacts to the TeamsMeetingService facade interface with a no-op binding off-enterprise; export from EE lib index.

Appointment approval path: inside the existing createdMeeting block, create the Online Meeting interaction (via trx helper) and the online_meetings row linking appointment_request_id + interaction_id.
Appointment approval path: keep writing appointment_requests.online_meeting_* columns (email/portal still read them).
Reschedule (updateAppointmentRequestDateTime): update the calendar event via provider_event_id and keep online_meetings + interaction times in sync.
Decline/cancel paths: set online_meetings.status = 'cancelled' and stop capture/polling.

Update/delete branch explicitly on presence of provider_event_id: new rows use the events endpoint; legacy rows (online_meeting_id only) keep standalone onlineMeeting handling or show a controlled warning.
All Graph create/update/delete calls run OUTSIDE DB transactions (move the reschedule updateTeamsMeeting call out of withTransaction).
Compensation: if Graph event creation succeeds but the subsequent DB transaction fails, delete/cancel the orphaned calendar event.
No backfill: pre-existing approved appointments get no online_meetings row or interaction; existing columns/links untouched.
scheduleTeamsMeeting action (withAuth + explicit hasPermission): inputs subject, start/end, organizer default, and one of contact/client and/or schedule entry; calls the facade (calendar-backed).
scheduleTeamsMeeting creates the Online Meeting interaction + online_meetings row via the shared helper.
scheduleTeamsMeeting optionally creates a schedule_entries row with work_item_type 'interaction' and work_item_id = created interaction id, linked via online_meetings.schedule_entry_id.
QuickAddInteraction: when type = Online Meeting, show a capability-gated 'Create Teams meeting' toggle with start/end; on save call scheduleTeamsMeeting.
EntryPopup: add a 'Generate Teams meeting' option when creating a schedule entry directly, reusing the existing teamsMeetingCapability plumbing.
Calendar-sync: write the join URL into the schedule entry notes (or add a field mapping) in BOTH eventMapping.ts copies so it appears in the pushed external event.
Shared fetch handler (no withAuth/session): resolves meeting, calls fetchMeetingArtifacts, upserts one online_meeting_artifacts row per artifact.

Fetch handler stores transcript content via an INTERNAL document helper (buffer/string + explicit tenant/user) — not the withAuth uploadDocument — associated to client + contact, is_client_visible=false unless expose_recordings_in_portal is on.
Fetch handler records recording artifacts with content_url; when download_recordings is on, also streams the blob into the file store and sets the artifact file_id.
Fetch handler sets online_meetings.status (recording_ready when any artifact exists; no_recording after the recording_fetch_attempts cap) and refreshes the interaction.
Fetch handler is idempotent: re-run upserts artifacts and skips transcript document creation when an artifact with a document_id already exists for that provider_artifact_id.
Recording proxy route (internal, authenticated, tenant-scoped, streaming/backpressure): resolves the artifact content_url server-side with the app token; never exposes the raw Graph URL.
InteractionModel.getById/getForEntity join online_meetings on (tenant, interaction_id) and its artifacts, populating IInteraction.online_meeting with an artifacts array.

InteractionDetails: 'Online Meeting' section with Join, status, 'Refresh recordings' button, and an artifacts list (transcripts -> View transcript document; recordings -> Download recording via proxy).
InteractionDetails uses InteractionIcon (Online Meeting -> video) and renders 'Recording pending' / 'No recording' states.
Appointment/portal views (EntryPopup, AppointmentRequestDetailsPage, AppointmentsPage) show recording/transcript links next to Join, gated on expose_recordings_in_portal (default MSP-only).
All new interactive elements have stable kebab-case ids and all new copy uses t('...') i18n keys.
EE migration: add default_meeting_organizer_object_id, download_recordings (default false), expose_recordings_in_portal (default false) to teams_integrations.

Move the organizer setting UI from AvailabilitySettings into the Teams integration settings page (UI move only; value already on teams_integrations); resolve and persist organizer AAD object id on save.
Tenant-settings UI controls for download_recordings and expose_recordings_in_portal in the Teams integration settings page.
Capability check (meetingCapabilityActions) gains a recordingsAvailable flag; UI warns when recording/transcript consent or Exchange-side mailbox scoping is missing instead of failing silently.

Setup docs: document Calendars.ReadWrite (application) WITH Exchange Application Access Policy/RBAC scoping to the organizer mailbox, plus the protected/metered recording & transcript permissions and Application Access Policy.
Feature gating: online_meetings, artifacts, and the interaction type are edition-agnostic; capture, subscriptions, and jobs gated on isEnterprise; facade no-ops off-enterprise.
[Phase 2] Create tenant-wide Graph change-notification subscriptions on getAllRecordings and getAllTranscripts; persist subscription id/expiry on teams_integrations.
[Phase 2] Recurring renewal job (scheduleRecurringJob, isEnterprise-gated) renews/recreates expiring subscriptions, mirroring the Microsoft email webhook renewal.
[Phase 2] Webhook route /api/teams/webhooks/recordings: echo validationToken, respond fast, enqueue a job that calls the shared fetch handler.

[Phase 2] clientState carries tenant/subscription routing only; the specific meeting is resolved from notification resourceData/@odata.id matched to provider_meeting_id; decrypt encrypted resource data.
